And the other side is a low pressure low temperature side.
And you have two components in any system that separate
the high and low pressure sides.
And that's the compressor and the metering device.
So the compressor's job is to increase the pressure
of the refrigerator in order to increase the temperature.
The metering device's job is to lower the refrigerant's pressure
in order to lower the temperature of the refrigerator.
And so you have your low pressure, low temperature vapor
refrigerant enters into a refrigerant compressor.

As the refrigerator enters into the coil,

And saturated means liquid and vapor both exist at the same time.
Anytime the refrigerator is saturated,
you have a phase change occurring.
And the phase change is the big secret
to allowing the refrigerant to absorb heat energy
from the air crossing the coil.
And that is because anytime that the refrigerator is saturated
where liquid and vapor exist, it's always
the same temperature as it travels through the coil
until, in this case, it becomes a vapor state,

And then the cycle starts all over again.
It's also important to make sure that you also
